headline: Ninja’s microwave air fryer could be the fix for soggy reheated pizza
summary: Ninja has launched its first microwave, the Crispi Microwave. This new appliance uniquely doubles as an air fryer. It aims to eliminate soggy reheated food, perfect for leftover pizza.

headline: Samsung's Galaxy Z Flip 8 leaks a week before launch event
summary: Leaked images and specifications for the upcoming Galaxy Z Flip 8 have surfaced. The new model appears nearly identical to the current Galaxy Z Flip 7. No significant upgrades are expected, prompting questions about the launch.

headline: OnePlus never had a chance in the US
summary: OnePlus has announced its exit from the United States market after an eight-year presence. Despite recent well-received products, the brand faced persistent challenges. Factors like T-Mobile discontinuing stock signaled its eventual departure.

headline: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
summary: 1Password has launched a new browser integration for the Anthropic chatbot, Claude. This feature allows Claude to access stored security credentials like usernames and passwords. Users can now authorize Claude to complete multi-step online tasks automatically.

headline: Clean the Squishies Codes for July 2026
summary: New codes are now available for the game 'Clean the Squishies'. Players can redeem these codes for free in-game rewards. The rewards specifically include various squishies.

headline: Anime Expeditions Codes for July 2026
summary: Fresh redemption codes have been released for the game 'Anime Expeditions'. These codes offer players free in-game rewards. Rewards include valuable trait crystals and gems.

headline: Great New Games Are Getting Caught Up in the Online Backlash Against PlayStation Killing Discs
summary: Angry gamers are protesting PlayStation's perceived move away from physical discs. This backlash is negatively impacting sales of otherwise excellent new indie games. Fans are encouraging purchases on non-PlayStation platforms in protest.

headline: Pokémon Go Fans React in Disgust as Players Hand-Picked to Attend Exclusive Times Square Event Sell Their Prized Mewtwo for Thousands of Dollars, Breaking the Game's Rules
summary: Pokémon Go players are outraged after an exclusive Mewtwo from a Times Square event was sold. Players hand-picked for the event sold the unique Mewtwo for thousands of dollars on eBay. This action is seen as breaking the game's established rules and fairness.

headline: Teenagers from 15 should be given free MenB vaccine, say UK experts
summary: UK medical experts recommend routine MenB vaccination for secondary school-aged teenagers. Advisers are urging the government to introduce free jabs for those aged 15 and above. The proposal aims to provide better protection against MenB disease.

headline: UK says Falkland Islands 'definitely ours' after Argentina banner
summary: The UK government has firmly reiterated its claim over the Falkland Islands. This declaration comes after Argentina displayed a banner during World Cup celebrations. Argentina could face discipline from Fifa regarding the celebratory display.

headline: Feeling gutted? Five ways to cope with England's World Cup defeat
summary: England's recent World Cup defeat has left many fans feeling disheartened. The article offers five practical tips for managing the disappointment. Experts and fans share strategies to cope with sports-related emotional lows.

headline: Why you should care about the third-place play-off between England and France
summary: This article argues for the relevance of the World Cup's third-place play-off match. It challenges the common perception that the game is insignificant. Reasons are provided to convince viewers to watch the England vs. France encounter.
